trends.name: Generative AI as Business Fulcrum
trends.description: Generative AI becoming the central organizing principle for marketing technology, data strategy, and customer engagement — not a feature but the foundation.

signals.name: AI-Driven Personalization at Individual Scale
signals.description: Shift from segment-based to truly individual-level personalization becoming technically achievable for mainstream businesses, not just platform giants.

signals.name: Privacy-Safe Data Infrastructure Mandate
signals.description: Privacy regulations and cookie deprecation forcing brands to build first-party data infrastructure as the foundational asset for all marketing and AI operations.

trends.name: AI Ubiquity Across Mobile Applications
trends.description: AI functionality embedding into nearly every mobile app category simultaneously — not as a feature but as baseline infrastructure for personalization, discovery, and engagement.

trends.name: Social Commerce Becoming Primary Mobile Revenue Model
trends.description: TikTok-popularized in-app tipping and direct creator payments reshaping mobile monetization, with subscriptions and in-app purchases overtaking traditional advertising in key categories.

trends.name: Mobile as IRL Companion
trends.description: Mobile devices becoming essential infrastructure for physical-world experiences — concerts, sports, travel — deepening the integration of digital and physical life.

signals.name: Non-Gaming Apps Driving Mobile Growth
signals.description: Streaming, dating, and UGC apps driving 11% YoY consumer spending growth in mobile, with gaming growth lagging for the first time — indicating maturation of entertainment app economy.

signals.name: Revenge Travel Driving Mobile Engagement
signals.description: Post-pandemic travel surge driving significant mobile activity in booking, experience, and documentation — with Swiftie and sports fan travel creating new demand spikes.

entries.filename: DCDX_Gen Z Top 25 Most Magnetic Brands 2023.pdf
DELOITTE - A study of AI's environmental footprint .pdf

trends.name: AI's Environmental Footprint as Strategic Risk
trends.description: Energy and water demands of AI infrastructure becoming a material ESG and operational risk factor, linking AI ambition to climate commitments in ways organizations cannot ignore.

signals.name: Data Center Energy Demand Surge
signals.description: AI-driven data center expansion creating significant new electricity demand that threatens to reverse progress on grid decarbonization in key markets.

signals.name: Green AI Policy Emerging for Data Centers
signals.description: First regulatory and voluntary frameworks specifically addressing AI infrastructure's environmental impact beginning to appear, foreshadowing compliance requirements.

trends.name: AI as Universal Infrastructure Layer
trends.description: AI transitioning from application to infrastructure — becoming as invisible and expected as HTTP or electricity, embedded in the substructure of all technology operations.

trends.name: Spatial Computing Taking Center Stage
trends.description: Spatial computing moving from consumer novelty to enterprise operational tool, enabling new human-machine interaction paradigms across healthcare, manufacturing, and design.

trends.name: Quantum Computing for Cryptography
trends.description: Quantum advances forcing organizations to begin migrating to post-quantum cryptographic standards before quantum computers make current encryption vulnerable.

trends.name: Hardware Eating the World
trends.description: Custom silicon, specialized chips, and purpose-built hardware becoming strategic competitive assets as software alone no longer differentiates AI performance.

signals.name: AI Elevating IT Function's Strategic Remit
signals.description: AI capabilities elevating the technology function from operational support to strategic business driver, reshaping the CTO/CIO role and its relationship to the board.

signals.name: Core Systems Modernization as AI Prerequisite
signals.description: Organizations realizing legacy core systems are blocking AI adoption — modernization projects accelerating not for their own sake but to enable AI deployment.

trends.name: Boundaryless Work Organizations
trends.description: Traditional organizational boundaries between departments, companies, and worker classifications dissolving as talent, work, and accountability flow across structural lines.

trends.name: Human Sustainability as Business Imperative
trends.description: Organizations recognizing that worker wellbeing, dignity, and flourishing are prerequisites for sustainable business performance — not trade-offs against it.

signals.name: Traditional Productivity Metrics Failing
signals.description: Standard productivity measurements becoming inadequate for knowledge work environments where AI augmentation, collaboration, and adaptability drive value creation.

signals.name: Transparency Paradox in Workplace Trust
signals.description: Research showing that more data transparency about worker performance and organizational decisions can sometimes reduce rather than increase trust — complicating HR analytics.

trends.name: EV Transition Pace Slower Than Forecast
trends.description: Consumer interest in electric vehicles decelerating from peak levels as range anxiety, charging infrastructure gaps, and price premiums persist despite technological improvement.

trends.name: Connected Vehicle as Platform
trends.description: Automotive industry restructuring around connected vehicle software and data services as the primary value creation layer, with hardware becoming commoditized.

signals.name: Shared Mobility Adoption Plateauing
signals.description: Consumer adoption of ride-sharing and mobility-as-a-service showing slower growth than forecast, with personal vehicle ownership remaining dominant preference globally.

signals.name: Consumer Autonomy Interest Stalling on Safety
signals.description: Consumer interest in full autonomous vehicles plateauing amid persistent safety concerns and high-profile incidents, slowing regulatory and commercial deployment timelines.

trends.name: Profitable Volume Over Revenue Growth
trends.description: Consumer goods companies shifting from price-driven revenue maximization to 'profitable volume' strategies — growing units sold at sustainable margins rather than premium pricing.

trends.name: GLP-1 Drugs Reshaping Consumer Products
trends.description: GLP-1 weight loss drugs beginning to structurally reshape food, beverage, and fitness consumption patterns — a pharmaceutical signal with broad consumer products implications.

signals.name: Price-Focused Era Ending for Consumer Goods
signals.description: Consumer goods companies reaching the limits of price increase tolerance — retailers and consumers pushing back, forcing return to volume and innovation competition.

trends.name: Consumer-Centric Health System Transformation
trends.description: Healthcare restructuring around empowered consumers who use AI, retail health, and digital tools to navigate and direct their own care — shifting power from providers to patients.

trends.name: AI Shifting Medical Power to Patients
trends.description: Generative AI giving consumers access to medical knowledge previously gated behind professional consultation, fundamentally altering the patient-clinician relationship.

signals.name: 16% of Patients Using AI to Understand Treatment Options
signals.description: Significant early adoption of AI tools by patients for medical decision support, pointing toward a future where AI is a routine part of health navigation.

signals.name: Retail Health Expansion into Primary Care
signals.description: Retail chains and pharmacy networks expanding into primary care delivery, disrupting the traditional healthcare provider model with convenience-first access points.

scenarios.name: The New Health CEO
scenarios.description: A future where consumers function as their own health executives — directing care pathways, evaluating providers, and using AI tools to make informed medical decisions with unprecedented autonomy.
